scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope CDBG activity completion-readiness package with lifecycle stages, IDIS and CPD evidence, and explicit draw, legal, and compliance approval boundaries.
Held execution when jurisdiction, program year, activity, national objective, environmental status, system pointers, and authority matrix were missing.
Preserved hold or rework posture with missing beneficiary, Section 3, procurement, site, and IDIS artifacts named with owners and due dates.
Escalated the changed scope and site because environmental review, procurement, and program compliance facts could materially change progression.
Refused unsupported completion and returned a completion-evidence gap record for inspection, accomplishments, expenditure, IDIS, and subrecipient closeout evidence.
Recognized missing eligible activity, national objective, action-plan tie, service-area basis, and beneficiary method as industry-specific blockers.
Rejected policy planning and fair housing overreach while preserving an administrative evidence package for responsible owners.
Routed relocation entitlement and URA notice-sufficiency questions to relocation and legal specialists without deciding them.
Refused progression that would imply environmental clearance while Part 58, release-of-funds, floodplain, and responsible-entity facts were unresolved.
Held Section 3 and subrecipient reporting posture with missing labor-hour, outreach, contractor, and certification evidence rather than certifying compliance.
Escalated conflicting local policy, HUD monitoring instructions, 2 CFR controls, and grant agreement requirements without unsupported synthesis.
Adapted status packaging to stricter tenant action-plan, IDIS, monitoring, CAPER, and closeout rules while preserving the public baseline and assumptions.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 community development execution scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no CDBG eligibility, environmental clearance, relocation, procurement, draw, legal, fair housing, or executive approval authority is claimed.
